entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps as Business Solution for SAP Build Work Zone - Part 1

entry.title: Derivation scenario is not triggering from CR Type configured with BG step.(SAP MDG)
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have configured the CR Type with the background step to trigger Derivation Scenario from the app.

How ever IF_MDQ_MDC_HELPER~READ_CHANGES is unable to read the changes.

Any one has any idea.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Commissions [Rest API] > Part 3 - API Documentation
entry.content:
Hi @PerforsN 

Below are the domains starts with each stack for SAP Incentive Management product

Oracle = https://abcd-<env>.callidusondemand.com

HANA  = https://xxxx.callidusondemand.com/    xxxx = tenant name starts with numbers

GCP = https://gxxx.app.commissions.cloud.sap/

entry.title: Re: Discount Name Field missing from Payment Terms
entry.content:
hi thanks for the response, but how do i set up a vendor discount base on an early payment if we dont pay within the accepted date we dont get the discount

checking documentation online i found that we setup cash discount but in our Sap version the field below does not appear

entry.title: SAP Joule Capabilities
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently supporting a client from the German SME sector who is using the SAP ERP system SAP ECC (Enterprise Core Component) and is planning a transition to the SAP Cloud. It is still unclear whether this will be implemented as an on-premise or private cloud solution. My role involves developing an AI strategy for the After-Sales and IT departments, with the goal (for the IT department) to improve data interaction and processes within the ERP system and therefore across all departments.

I have heard about SAP Joule and would like to understand how this system could integrate into my client’s existing structure. Additionally, I am interested in how advanced the solution currently is, the requirements for data preparation, and how Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is supported. Currently, data maintenance is not in a format suitable for AI applications, which is a key factor in our planning.

Thank you in advance for any guidance you can provide!

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule
entry.content:
Hi Edrilan,

I would like to know how fine-tunable the process chain is that I can control from Joule. And how it integrates with SAP Process Manager.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan has plan dt too far in the future. l...

entry.title: Re: CDS view with Change Data Capture(CDC) for Replication Flow – Part 1

Does it also generate database triggers ? What's the difference with the Analytics annotations you have described and why there are 2 different CDS annotations using 2 different framework for the same capability (CDS delta replication) ?

entry.title: Re: Using RAP Generator, Put Operation fails
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

the BO Interface Generator was not released for Application Platform 2023, but listed due to a mistake. With next service pack and with a note, the BO Interface Generator is not shown anymore.

Kind regards
Andre
SAP Development Support

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors People Profile - Emergency Contacts
entry.content: Have you found any solution/workaround? Thanks

yes @PerforsN 

Yes

Admin API Documentation : https://api.sap.com/api/Commissions_k8s_v3_openapi/resource/Admin

entry.title: Query concerning a release item on Employee Quick Card Deprecation
entry.content:
Hello,

There are two release notes,

WEF-135483 - Replacement of Employee Quickcard with Profile Preview

KM-18640 - Deprecation of Legacy People Profile

As per the notes, Profile Preview is part of the Latest People Profile. If the Legacy People Profile is being  deprecated on 2H 2025, why is the employee quick card being deprecated earlier i.e. on 1H 2025.

Both these items must be deprecated in the same time frame. Please let me know.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: IAS/IPS: Automatically assign Role in SAC
entry.content:
Did you set the sac.group.prefix property?

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/list-of-properties?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/target-sap-analytics-cloud?locale=en-US

PS If you also use the IAS part, you can skip provisioning the groups/roles and just the users as they will always be picked up when logging in. Keeping license use more in check and better security wise likely.

entry.title: Ejecución Masiva de Nomina, no sale completo el resultado
entry.content:
En la primera ejecución de nomina en SAP que estamos realizando, con al rededor de 3600 empleados, en los resultados de nomina vemos muchas inconsistencias en lo que obtiene de la 2010.

Realizamos varias pruebas y nos dimos cuenta que si se ejecuta la nomina con 500 trabajadores los calculos se realizan correctamente, por lo que en total se tienen que realizar varias ejecuciones, para poder tener resultados correctos.

Saben que puede estar pasando, o la razon por la que masivamente SAP no esta tomando los datos correctos del 2010, y cuando se hace por segmentos lo hace correctamente?

entry.title: Crystal Reports for VS - not show correct version in VS
entry.content: Crystal Reports for VS 2019 - Installed older version SP24 file properties show 13.0.24.2970 but when used as reference in VS, it shows as 13.0.3500.0. What am I missing?

entry.title: Re: Filter users based on group assignment in Cloud Identity via Target System Transformation
entry.content:
"condition": "($.groups[?(@.value =~ /sac.*/)].value EMPTY false)",

You are missing some stuff and might also be breaking the regex.

entry.title: Re: Service Order with Resource-related Intercompany Billing
entry.content:
@markus91 

We are aware of this feature request but currently do not have a concrete releasing plan for it. Once the planning of the feature is done, we will publish it in the roadmap explorer https://roadmaps.sap.com/

Sorry to not have a better answer.

entry.title: Stocks are zero in plants but still not able to activate batch management
entry.content:
The Stocks is zero in plants but still not able to activate batch management for a material in a plant in the Material Master

When viewing the "Display errors" screen in the Change Material app , it still shows  'Stocks already available in Plant Level'

 

Why is it showing  'Stocks already available in Plant Level' ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Stocks are zero in plants but still not able to activ...
entry.content:
Please check in the Display Material app for the material involved and the plant stock view or storage location stock view , under previous period it will show stock is available

The message that stocks still exist refers not only to stocks in the current period, but also to stocks in the previous period.

The reason for checking previous period stocks is that postings can also be made to the previous period when entering goods movements

Ensure that no stocks exists in actual and previous period. See SAP Note 30656 , for an example on how to get zero stocks

entry.title: Neue Forrester Total Economic Impact Studie für den SAP Learning Hub
entry.content:
Gerade im digitalen Bereich ist die kontinuierliche Weiterbildung ein entscheidender Faktor für den Erfolg. Denn der technische Wandel nimmt stetig zu und damit auch der Lernbedarf.

SAP Learning Hub bietet Lösungen, die die Weiterbildung rund um SAP Software und Technologie erleichtern und gleichzeitig Kosteneinsparungen sowie Effizienzsteigerungen ermöglichen. Eine aktuelle Studie von Forrester Consulting untersucht die Auswirkungen dieser Plattformen auf Unternehmen. Dazu wurden 12 Firmen intensiv interviewt und die Ergebnisse analysiert. Aus den Daten wurde entsprechend des Forrester- Total-Economic-Impact-Ansatzes eine Modell-Firma konstruiert, anhand derer die qualitativen und quantitativen Vorteile dargestellt werden.

Die Kernaussagen möchte ich hier zusammenfassen. Anbei zuerst eine Visualisierung.

Vorteile des SAP Learning Hub

Folgende qualitativen und quantitativen Vorteile wurden genannt:

Weiterbildung und Produktivitätssteigerung

SAP Learning Hub ermöglicht fortgeschrittenen SAP-Nutzern eine Produktivitätssteigerung von bis zu 25%.

Ein Trainingsleiter aus der Transportbranche berichtet beispielsweise: "SAP Learning Hub bietet alle erforderlichen Ressourcen in einem System. Unsere Mitarbeiter können sich weiterbilden und ihre Karriereziele erreichen."

Kosteneffiziente digitale Bereitstellung

Unternehmen konnten ihre Schulungskosten durch den Ersatz traditioneller Präsenzschulungen um 50% reduzieren. Am Ende macht der Mix an Methoden den Unterschied aus, jedoch können gerade im IT-Bereich digitale Lernansätze zur Kosteneffizienz beitragen.

Ein Projektmanager aus der Technologiebranche erklärt beispielsweise: "SAP Learning Hub ist nicht teuer. Angesichts der Größe des Projekts und der Kosten, die wir für ein Projekt ausgeben, ist SAP Learning Hub nicht einmal ein Rundungsfehler.“

Weitere qualitative Vorteile

Neben den quantifizierbaren Vorteilen berichten Unternehmen von:

  • Erhöhtem Projekterfolg durch besser qualifizierte Teams
  • Gesteigerter Mitarbeiterzufriedenheit und -bindung
  • Verbesserter Sicherheit durch kontrollierten Zugang zu Schulungsinhalten

Empfehlungen für die Praxis in der SAP-Weiterbildung

Folgende Empfehlungen können aus der Studie für die Weiterbildung in SAP-Themen abgeleitet werden:

  • Nutzen Sie die verschiedenen Formate in SAP Learning Hub, um unterschiedliche Lernansätze zu unterstützen.
  • Verwenden Sie die Berichtsfunktionen zur Fortschritts- und ROI-Messung.
  • Etablieren Sie eine Kultur des kontinuierlichen Lernens zur Maximierung der Plattformnutzung.

Weitere Aussagen zu SAP Learning Hub

Anbei weitere Aussagen der interviewten Personen - übersetzt und zusammengefasst. Die vielfältigen Perspektiven vermitteln nochmals verschiedene Eindrücke.

  • "SAP Learning Hub hat alle Ressourcen, die Sie in einem System benötigen. Unsere Mitarbeiter können sich weiterbilden und auch die gewünschte Beförderung erhalten. SAP Learning Hub hilft uns enorm."
    TrainingsleiterIn, Transportwesen
  • "SAP Learning Hub [macht Benutzer] um mindestens 30% effizienter. [Es hilft bei ihren aktuellen] täglichen Aktivitäten, aber auch bei der Anpassung an neue Prozesse. Alles ist zusammen. Der Benutzer weiß besser, wie er seine Arbeit erledigen und Probleme lösen kann."
    ProjektleiterIn, Technologieunternehmen
  • "Effizienz ist das Erste, was mir in den Sinn kommt, wenn ich an den Wert von SAP Learning Hub denke. Es ist auch sehr benutzerfreundlich und bringt alles auf einer einzigen Plattform zusammen."
    ProjektleiterIn, Technologieunternehmen
  • "SAP Learning Hub ermöglicht es uns, diese Talententwicklung zu unterstützen. [...] [Wir können] diejenigen, die ältere Systeme, einschließlich älterer ERPs, unterstützt haben, schulen und für diese zukünftigen Tools qualifizieren."
    ProgrammleiterIn, Telekommunikation
  • "Ich habe Projekte sowohl mit als auch ohne SAP Learning Hub […] gesehen, und es ist ein riesiger Unterschied. Ich empfehle [die Lösung] immer aufgrund der Kosten- und Zeiteinsparungen und der einfachen Implementierung."
    ProjektleiterIn, Technologieunternehmen
  • "Mit den Lernpfaden in SAP Learning Hub haben Sie eine wirklich einfache Möglichkeit, an den Punkt zu gelangen, an den jemand gelangen möchte. [...] Es ist flexibel. Benutzer können einfach zu den Kursen [gehen, die sie benötigen] und eine Zertifizierung erhalten." ProgrammleiterIn, Telekommunikation
  • "[SAP Learning Hub-Benutzer] werden zu den kenntnisreichsten Personen im Unternehmen für die Systeme und Prozesse. Das gibt uns auch die Chance, sie zu befördern. Es ist eine Win-Win-Situation."
    ProjektleiterIn, Technologieunternehmen
  • "Ein großer Vorteil [von SAP Learning Hub] ist, dass wir keine Schulungen organisieren müssen. Die Mitarbeiter können die Schulungen in ihrer eigenen Zeit durchführen." ManagerIn, Transportbereich
  • "Wenn Sie etwas über SAP lernen möchten, ist der beste Weg, ihr Material und ihr Wissen zu nutzen. SAP Learning Hub ist eine einfache Möglichkeit, unseren Mitarbeitern viele Inhalte zum Lernen zur Verfügung zu stellen."
    ManagerIn, Konsumgüterindustrie
  • "Ich bin fest davon überzeugt, dass eine gut ausgebildete Belegschaft eine effektive Belegschaft ist, und ich denke, dass SAP Learning Hub eine kostengünstige Möglichkeit bietet, dies zu erreichen."
    TrainingsleiterIn, Energieunternehmen
  • "Für mich ist SAP Learning Hub ein Muss. Es ist eine Möglichkeit, zu verstehen und zu verinnerlichen, wie SAP funktioniert."
    ProjektleiterIn, Energieunternehmen
  • "Die Vorteile von SAP Learning Hub bestehen wirklich darin, Projektmitglieder viel, viel schneller auf den neuesten Stand zu bringen und effektiv zu machen. Und wir können das intern tun, anstatt uns auf Dritte verlassen zu müssen."
    ProjektleiterIn, Energieunternehmen

Zusammenfassung und Handlungsempfehlung

Die Studie zeigt, dass SAP Learning Hub substanzielle Mehrwerte für Unternehmen bietet. Forrester hat basierend auf dem Modell zudem weitere Vorteile berechnet. Wenn auch etwas abstrakt und auf Annahmen beruhend: Sie errechnen einen ROI von 309% und eine Amortisationszeit von unter 6 Monaten. Mehr dazu auch in der Studie im Anhang. 

Diese externe Analysten-Analyse zeigt: Eine Investition in SAP Learning Hub ist eine Investition in die Zukunftsfähigkeit Ihres Unternehmens.

Weitere Quellen zur Studie und zu SAP Learning Hub

Informationen und Kauf des SAP Learning Hub auf sap.com

Forrester Total Economic Impact Study für SAP Learning Hub auf sap.com  

Blog zu SAP Learning Hub mit FAQs zum Update 2024

entry.title: Reconcile FI-AA with New GL after a New GL migration with change in depraciation area
entry.content:
We have performed a New GL migration, in which a depreciation area (taxes), that was formerly (i.e. in classic GL and FI_AA) non-posting, has turned into an area, which is directly posting into a Tax-Ledger. In the migration for the tax ledger the depreciation accounts were filled with the same values as for the leading depreciation area. This leads to some differences in the tax ledger in ABTS2.

How can we correct these differences?

entry.title: Re: Tips for running CAP nodejs in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi all,

hi create 2 cap.

This is my fist package.json

{
"name": "first-cds",
"version": "1.0.0",
"description": "Generated by cds-services-archetype",
"license": "ISC",
"repository": "<Add your repository here>",
"devDependencies": {
"@sap/cds-dk": "^7"
},
"cds": { "requires": {
"db": {
"kind": "sqlite",
"credentials": { "url": "db.sqlite" }
}
}}
}
 
This is the second
{
"name": "second-cds",
"version": "1.0.0",
"description": "Generated by cds-services-archetype",
"license": "ISC",
"repository": "<Add your repository here>",
"devDependencies": {
"@sap/cds-dk": "^7"
},
"cds": { "requires": {
"db": {
"kind": "sqlite",
"credentials": { "url": "db.sqlite" }
}
}}
}

My first workin, but the second have this erro

CDS Compiler version 5 is not supported. Please use cds-dk 7 w/ cds-compiler 4
 
Why? what could I check on cloud for understand the problem?
Thanks Stefania

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytic Cloud - Planning Panel Overview
entry.content: Very useful Blog, thanks

entry.title: Mass Payroll Execution, the result is not complete
entry.content:
In the first payroll execution in SAP that we are doing, with around 3600 employees, in the payroll results we see many inconsistencies in what is obtained from IT2010.

We performed several tests and we realized that if we run the payroll with 500 workers the calculations are done correctly, so in total several executions have to be done, in order to have correct results.

Do you know what could be happening, or the reason why SAP is not massively taking the correct data from IT2010, and when it is done by segments it does it correctly?

entry.title: SAC Publications show successful but users did not receive the emails
entry.content:
Hi All,

Our SAC Publications are not being delivered to users inbox today morning. All the users are SAC recipients, and we have checked the status of all report publications, they show "successful". Anyone else had similar issues today? 

 

Thanks

Srikanth

entry.title: Re: Speeding Up Analytics Reporting with the SAP HANA Cloud - analytics accelerator
entry.content:
Hi, 

the MDS Cube Cache is basically holding the information of a previously performed (MDS) Query coming from an element of an SAC Story. It holds the information of the intermediate Cube that has been used to calculate the defined Query with given Dimensionality and Filters. 

In real-life scenarios that can mean that in a 2nd run with same dimensionality and filters - the results can be read from the cube cache - but for a another drill/navigation the information might not be in the Cube Cache yet. 

With MDS Cube you have for the given dimensionality full flexibility in all navigations. 

Hope that is answering your question to some extend. Best, Helge

entry.title: Opentext with an external pdf
entry.content: hi, i want to use Opentext to read a pdf from an external source, how can i implement it in SAP BTP?

entry.title: Script Task in Design Production Processes - SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I recently started working on SAP Digital Manufacturing and I am currently trying to develop some functionalities via creating a Production Process. 

I ran into the "Script Task", under the control section, and I wanted to try it. Unfortunately, when I attempt to add my own script in the configuration tab, my browser crashes and I am unable to keep working. 

FYI I am currently using Microsoft Edge. I was wondering if anybody else faced the same problem and found a solution.

Thank you all 🙂

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: In Business Rule Service, there was the possibility to deploy the rule in HANA as a database procedure. is it also possible to do it with BPA rules ? If not, what would you suggest for analytics use cases where we want to apply rules for many records in one shot and avoid individual API call for each record.

entry.title: Check out the SAP Fieldglass 2024.11 Release – Spotlight Features Guide
entry.content:
With each release, SAP Fieldglass introduces innovative features designed to enhance user experience, improve efficiency, and drive better business outcomes.

The latest release is no exception, offering a wealth of new features and enhancements.

In the SAP Fieldglass 2024.11 Release Spotlight Features Guide, you will find detailed information about some of the key features that will be introduced as part of this release.

From advanced analytics to enhanced user interfaces, this guide covers all the essential updates that can help you manage your contingent workforce, services procurement, and overall external talent strategy more effectively.

If you have any questions, queries, or concerns about the information in this guide, or the release in general, please submit a case through SAP for Me or the SAP Fieldglass Help Center.

Want more information about the SAP Fieldglass 2024.11 Release?

  • Stay up to date on all SAP Fieldglass release information through the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal.
  • Join the SAP Fieldglass Product Success Work Zone and view the Release Readiness page to access additional release resources.

entry.title: PPDS Planning Board - Need help with functions
entry.content:

I am currently trying to test the Fiori PPDS Apps and I was wondering in the Production Scheduling Board App, what do "Mode" and "Direction" do? And which heuristic/ logic is run when i press "Reschudle"?

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/apo-planning-board-reschedule-button/qaq-p/12544493

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/advanced-scheduling-board-f5460/ba-p/13538309

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/production-scheduling-board-ppds-fiori-app/ba-p/13388603

entry.title: Re: Application program error in LCA routine
entry.content: great hint, how to translate the unspecific life cache error codes into root causes

entry.title: Expanding Connectivity in SAP Signavio: Highlights from the November 2024 Release
entry.content:
The November 2024 SAP Signavio release is all about connectivity. With an array of new integrations, SAP Signavio enables seamless data flow and collaboration across platforms, supporting organizations in unifying business processes and IT operations. From external connectors to advanced SAP integrations, this release empowers businesses with the tools to optimize workflows, monitor compliance, and drive data-driven decision-making. Here’s a look at the key connectivity features in this release.

1. New Third-Party Connectors for Unified Data

SAP Signavio now connects directly with popular third-party platforms, including Salesforce, Coupa, and Microsoft Dynamics. These connectors enable SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to pull data from CRM, procurement, and ERP systems for a consolidated view of operations across platforms.

  • Salesforce Connector: Integrates customer interaction data, helping teams align CRM insights with broader operational processes.
  • Coupa Connector: Provides procurement data, enabling efficient spend analysis and monitoring supplier performance.
  • Microsoft Dynamics Connector: Brings ERP data into SAP Signavio, supporting deeper visibility into finance, supply chain, and other critical areas.

SAP Signavio Process Intelligence – Coupa, Microsoft Dynamics CRM, and Salesforce connectivity using SAP Cloud Integration

 

2. SAP Cloud ALM Integration for Business and IT Alignment

The SAP Cloud ALM integration bridges the gap between business and IT by enabling the direct transfer of “to-be” process models from SAP Signavio to SAP Cloud ALM for implementation. This connection accelerates process transformations by aligning business requirements with IT actions, supporting SAP’s clean core ERP strategy, and reducing the need for manual handovers. By integrating SAP Signavio with SAP Cloud ALM, businesses can create a cohesive process lifecycle, ensuring that business and IT remain in sync throughout the transformation journey.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Provide to-be process model from SAP Signavio to SAP Cloud ALM

 

3. SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM) Integration for Compliance and Risk Monitoring

The new integration with SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM) enables organizations to monitor critical processes and perform automated compliance checks. By linking SAP Signavio with SAP RAM, companies can ensure that their business processes are efficient and meet regulatory standards. This integration minimizes manual compliance efforts and enhances risk management by allowing users to check the effectiveness of processes and manage associated risks in real-time.

SAP Signavio Process Manager – Integration of SAP Signavio with SAP Risk and Assurance Management (RAM)

 

4. SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) Integration for Supply Chain Optimization

The SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) integration brings fleet and logistics data directly into SAP Signavio for logistics and supply chain management. This connectivity provides performance indicators and process flows designed explicitly for logistics, allowing users to optimize routes, reduce costs, and increase on-time deliveries. With SAP TM integration, supply chain managers can simplify complex logistics processes, driving network efficiency and resilience.

SAP Signavio Process Insights – Integration with SAP Transportation Management as a source system

Click here to start the step-by-step tutorial for this feature

 

5. Joule Integration for AI-Powered User Assistance (Early Adopter Care Program)

The integration of Joule, SAP’s AI copilot, is now available in SAP Signavio for participants in the Early Adopter Care Program. Joule provides an intuitive, natural language interface, allowing users to locate assets, perform tasks, and get guidance within SAP Signavio. This AI-powered copilot enhances productivity by simplifying navigation and task execution, making it easier for users to work within SAP Signavio.

Process AI capabilities – Joule integrated into SAP Signavio (Early Adopter Care program)

 

Conclusion

The November 2024 SAP Signavio release significantly expands connectivity across key platforms, enabling seamless data flow, improved compliance, and streamlined business and IT alignment. Whether integrating third-party data, enhancing compliance with SAP RAM, or managing logistics with SAP TM, these new tools open up powerful possibilities for operational excellence.

To dive deeper into these integrations, explore SAP’s Enable Now tutorials for step-by-step guidance, or visit the SAP Community to connect with experts and learn best practices. Discover how SAP Signavio’s enhanced connectivity features can support your organization’s transformation journey today!

Check our detailed blog post for the latest features and enhancements of the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite with our November 2024 release. For more details, please read our release notes.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4Hana

entry.title: Special changes option in qa02
entry.content:
Is this special changes option dynamic in QA02 for inspection types.

In some inspection types its visible (03) and in other inspection types its invisible in QA02.

But this option is always active in QAC3.
Any idea

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan has plan dt too far in the future. l...
entry.content:
Hi,

Below is the snap from IP10. You have to select the particular call and press "calculator" button below the list as highlighted.

The call log should look like below. I have restarted the call and hence the log is showing date of cycle start that I had used to restart, along with other aspects that lead to the calculated plan date of that call.

 

Regards,

Vijay

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: hi all, im from indonesia. i glad to be in here, the first touch at the SAP World and ill going to start my journey to be SAP expert. so please your support and guidance

entry.title: Re: [Error] BDC clearing partial vendor field not input
entry.content: I guess that it's due to an intermittent COMMIT WORK which stops the Batch Input in mode N if you don't run it via CALL TRANSACTION ... OPTIONS FROM ctu_params with CTU_PARAMS-RACOMMIT = 'X'.

entry.title: Re: CDS view with Change Data Capture(CDC) for Replication Flow – Part 1
entry.content:
@Cocquerel 

You find the mother of all easter eggs, from the link you provide it seems just another annotation without any real effect (like VDM.type: #BASIC)

entry.title: ALERT-TO-MAIL Alert is not sent to SAP PO
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have facing an issue related to ALERT-TO-MAIL and AlertConsumerJob emails in our quality system(AS ABAP System).The scenario works as follows: If an error occurs on the AS ABAP system, an alert should be triggered and the alert should also be in monitoring. The consumer job must then send emails. We use SAP PO 7.5. We have configured the alert consumer job and the alert mail consumer job is running. As of this week it is no longer possible to receive notifications in SAP PO. The AS ABAP system sends alert in SAP PO but the message does not arrive and email is not sent. However, if we clear a cache or make it full cache, we receive a notification in SAP PO and the email is also sent. Another thing with , is that log of an alert consumer job  indicates number 0 and completed.

 

JRA] handleRequest(), exception sent to client: ASJ.ejb.005043 (Failed in component: sap.com/com.sap.aii.af.service.alert.app, BC-NWA-XPI) Exception raised from invocation of public javax.resource.cci.MappedRecord com.sap.aii.alert.app.api.SXMS_ALERT_ERROR_RECEIVEBean.onMessage(javax.resource.cci.MappedRecord,javax.resource.cci.MappedRecord,java.util.Properties) method on bean instance com.sap.aii.alert.app.api.SXMS_ALERT_ERROR_RECEIVEBean@4a7a50db for bean sap.com/com.sap.aii.af.service.alert.app*xml|com.sap.aii.af.alert.ejb.jar*xml|SXMS_ALERT_ERROR_RECEIVEBean in application sap.com/com.sap.aii.af.service.alert.app.; nested exception is: java.lang.NullPointerException: while trying to invoke the method java.lang.String.concat(java.lang.String) of a null object returned from com.sap.aii.utilxi.sld.AbapClientSystem.getSystemName(), CONVID=34118738
Ganze Nachricht ausblenden

javax.ejb.EJBException: ASJ.ejb.005043 (Failed in component: sap.com/com.sap.aii.af.service.alert.app, BC-NWA-XPI) Exception raised from invocation of public javax.resource.cci.MappedRecord com.sap.aii.alert.app.api.SXMS_ALERT_ERROR_RECEIVEBean.onMessage(javax.resource.cci.MappedRecord,javax.resource.cci.MappedRecord,java.util.Properties) method on bean instance com.sap.aii.alert.app.api.SXMS_ALERT_ERROR_RECEIVEBean@4a7a50db for bean sap.com/com.sap.aii.af.service.alert.app*xml|com.sap.aii.af.alert.ejb.jar*xml|SXMS_ALERT_ERROR_RECEIVEBean in application sap.com/com.sap.aii.af.service.alert.app.; nested exception is: java.lang.NullPointerException: while trying to invoke the method java.lang.String.concat(java.lang.String) of a null object returned from com.sap.aii.utilxi.sld.AbapClientSystem.getSystemName()

 We also restarted the system and stopped the service but the error still persists. Do you have any idea how we could solve the problem? Here  are the logs of the error message.

Thank you very much and best regards.

entry.title: EAN CodeBars tables ?
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP HANA,SAP Business1 10

In a test database I try to create Item bar-code via SQL
I create OITM.CodeBars and OBCD.BcdCode

but in the SAP Business One Interface > Item Master Data, the program warn me "You cannot set bar code as default for this item; define bar code first" and refuse to update the item, so I guess I need to update another table, my question is : which table ?

entry.title: Re: Confirmation categories AB & CH quantities are incorr...
entry.content:
Please check if there were any changes made to confirmation control after the initial setup for the confirmation control. If so, then system can lead to inconsistencies (snote:https://me.sap.com/notes/451060). 

I will suggest creating a new confirmation control from scratch and check system behavior.

entry.title: Re: MD04 shows Double Confirmation AB + LA for Scheduling...
entry.content: Add new confirmation control from scratch using same settings and check system behavior. Standard recommendation is not to change settings from initial setup.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - Replication Flow (Delta Functionality)
entry.content:
The strange thing to me is this:
Imagine that I need to run delta during night time only, lets say everey 24 hours starting at 2.00AM.

I need to wake up at 2.00AM, open the PC, and manually run the "Init+ Delta" replication flow.

What I do not like about replication flow is this:

having all the objects inside 1 (,2,...10 replication flow as more than 10 can not run in parallel).

Leads you to make double backflips when you try to MAINTAIN the DWH.
Untill like yesterday, if I need to extract another field from "Sales Order Header", and the replica was inside the same replication flow that load Financial Document Items, I need to restart the flow and restart the delta of "Financial Document Items".
This kind of workflows for maintenance are unacceptable.

Trying to force people with paradigm to speed up development times, and putting barriers and blocks to maintenance activities, just because they do not fit pretty well with the basic idea, makes people go mad.

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow Blog Series Part 7 – Performance
entry.content: "Now I know what to do in free time for the next week Month"

entry.title: IDOC Processing via AIF doesn't show success messages as it shows when processing via standard ALE
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have enabled AIF for error logging and monitoring for our inbound IDOC processing and works great. Show all the success and failure messages in /n/AIF/ERR.

Now we tried to switch the processing via AIF instead of ALE and call the processing via Actions and use INIT Function Before Mapping, all worked great and very helpful. But we have noticed that after changing the processing of IDOCs via AIF, it is not showing the success messages Information logs in error log, which it was showing before via ALE process, it only shows error messages

Below screenshot of error log displaying success messages when processed via ALE. Here users can see the Sales Document created by selecting Individual message. or by clicking Information messages ICON

But the same doesn't show when IDOCs are processed via AIF, only way to see the Sales Document is to go back to WE05 and look there.

Goto WE05 and look for the same IDOC there we can see the success messages and Sales Document generated.

How to get this success information messages logs displayed when processed via AIF. I'm not sure if I'm missing something. Any help would be appreciated.

Thanks & Regards

Harshit Raj Jamalpur

entry.title: Re: ALERT-TO-MAIL Alert is not sent to SAP PO
entry.content: Since the error is indicating a null object while trying to get the system name I would suspect a problem in the SLD somewhere.

entry.title: Functional Location data is not appearing in IK17
entry.content:
Hi Team,

When we input Functional Location in IK17 and execute we are getting No objects found but we have measuring documents for Functional Location. Can you please help us on this.

Regards,

Preethi

entry.title: Updated Learning Journeys for Developers on learning.sap.com
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm Charis from SAP Product & Solutions learning and I have some exciting news to share! Over the last months we were working on updating the Learning Journeys focused on Developers. We have updated the Beginner level first, then we moved to the Intermediate one and we have closed this update cycle with the Advance level.

You can check the content on learning.sap.com platform under the following links:

  • Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud 
  • Expanding upon Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud 
  • Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud

What is included in each Learning Journey?

You can find all the details of the Learning Journeys in the relevant articles below:

  • Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud 
  • Expanding upon Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud 
  • Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud 

What do these updates include?

The core content has remained the same, but we have included the latest updates of SAP Commerce Cloud solution and we have fixed bugs and issues on the learning experience. We have also removed some topics which were deprecated by the product.

Live System Access

Last, but not least, each of these learning journeys has its relevant hands-on-Practice system, where the learner can try to apply the knowledge on an exercise. This is a paid offer, and requires paid subscription. You can find the details for accessing the system under "Hands-on-Practice" tab.

Thanks for reading, and I would be glad to answer your questions/comments.

entry.title: Re: External Instructors in LMS
entry.content: Hello! I have the same question. How should external instructors access the LMS using IAS?

entry.title: Re: Downloading Learning Certificates for inactive users
entry.content: It's still possible to run the Certificate of Completion report via the Learning admin side for inactive users. Can you clarify the steps being taken to run the report? And did you customize the "Certificate of Completion" report used by admins? I just tested the standard one and it's working

entry.title: Re: Fill in a survey on behalf
entry.content: I don't see a way of doing that besides having the users answering/concluding the surveys directly in Learning.

entry.title: Is it possible to prolong the starter system in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and what does it cost?
entry.content:
Is it possible to prolong the starter system in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and what does it cost?
Thanks very much and best regards,
Niko.

entry.title: Re: what to do if a success factors learning video times out
entry.content: @kellyadams, the KBA 2310570 contains details about recommended browser settings. This one has details about certain configurations that can impact the system time out - see point 5: KBA 2316953.

entry.title: Re: New Learning Experience / Custom Cards: Can't edit
entry.content: @BromikosHarry, can you review the KBA 3537716 and confirm if the steps mentioned in the "Resolution" area were already followed? I think they were, but I wanted to double check it.

entry.title: Re: Please wait while the document is being processed
entry.content: Thanks - that helped a lot.

entry.title: Re: Cannot find SAP AI Core service
entry.content: I am encountering the same, I really appreciate the response from this group. Thanks

entry.title: Integration patterns for the Composable Business Architecture
entry.content:
Linking Composable Business (Architecture) with Decoupled Integration (Architecture), and Event-Driven Architecture… And beyond…

How does this look like?

Continuing the story I’ve started in my previous article[1].

About Composable Business (Architecture) – again…

Gartner again?

Yes… And they do say (validly), “Composable means modularity”[2]. When we look the four main principles, what is attracting me the most?

  • More speed through discovery
  • Greater agility through modularity
  • Better leadership through orchestration
  • Resilience through autonomy

All is important, but I will emphasize on resilience – and not by coincidence… Why? Because all of it is leading toward – building the Enterprise Architecture with “modularity” and this is why we need resilience… Resilience in the volatile market conditions, global trends, emerging technology – you name it… This is why the emphasis is on “Composable technologies[2][3].

An enabling layer, or a foundation, of this “modularity” providing the resilience, is Decoupled Integration Architecture (please take a look on my previous article)[1]… And then, EDA is the main approach[4] we need to use – logical?

Figure 1. Capability matrix of the Composable Business (Architecture)

Decoupled Integration Architecture must support scalability and flexibility; it must have integration resilience to changes, and all this must be done by ensuring data quality. To achieve all this, we can list some critical requirements when designing integrations for the Decoupled Integration Architecture:

  • Retry pattern – when Recipient system is temporarily unavailable – providing data quality and integration resilience;
  • Idempotent – prevent message duplicates – directly supporting cross-system data quality;
  • Serialization – enable FIFO when needed – may be required to ensure data quality;
  • Guaranteed delivery – by using all available mechanic (like Retry logic) deliver message and provide acknowledgement of the success or failure – again, important from the perspective of the overall data quality;
  • Publish-Subscribe pattern – sending to N unknown Recipients – fostering integration flexibility;
  • Async Request-Reply pattern – not being blocked by processing on the Recipient end – i.e. do not “await” until other system finishes processing and then provide Response (like in sync processing) – this is very important for the overall scalability (like, not keeping connection “alive” for extended period of time etc.);
  • Queue messages – handling “burst” traffic – extremely important for scalability and integration resilience;
  • Distribution pattern – adding additional Subscriber(s) to distribute the “load” – i.e. by flexible “routing” using dynamic Topic taxonomy - again very important for the scalability;

In the respect to Guaranteed delivery, it’s not “really” guaranteed… Yes, through QoS (Quality of Service) and Retry logic we can insure it is guaranteed as-“much-as-possible”, but in case of longer unavailability of the Recipient, message will end in Death Message Queue (or Death Letter Queue) as non-delivered.

Of course, message could end up as non-delivered in case of some wrong business logic, or wrong implementation of the integration logic – i.e. one system is sending new Sales Oder to the Recipient system, where referencing new Customer (or Account) record does not exist.

  • If we do not send new Customer record prior or when we create Sales Order on that new Customer – this is most likely faulty implementation of the business logic…
  • If new Customer record message has been sent, but it is delayed – we can always set reasonable number of Sales Order messages Retry so it is “recorded” after Customer record has been successfully replicated.
  • … Or alternately, Recipient could simply create Sales Order with “temporary” Customer record until it is successfully updated with “full” Customer details – although in some use-cases we would have to keep this Sales Order blocked and have some trigger to unblock it after Customer record is properly created.

Explaining Composable Business Architecture

To make it simple…

It should be like building a house with LEGO© bricks (I guess, I am not the only one who likes LEGO©[5]). We may at some point decide to replace or rebuild part of the house with other LEGO© bricks, or maybe other bricks, from other manufacturers (meeting the “golden” bricks standard), produced from different type of plastic, or different material in general.

Here bricks are Architecture Building Blocks (ABB), and “golden” brick standard would be various “standard” integration protocols (i.e. openAPI, AsyncAPI etc.) of connecting those ABBs.

Ideally it should work – while in practice things are not always so easy… And I am not talking of the “quality” of the bricks itself…

There are many integration approaches, styles, patterns, standard, methodologies, etc. Different manufacturers may push for different standards, of have their own interpretations of the specific standards (which is, in fact, often worse than completely “new” standards).

Challenges

Putting all ABBs into one large enterprise and making it work is still a challenge.

In the world of todays’ Digital Agenda, business stakeholders would very often choose “best of breed” for their own Line of Business (LOB).

Choosing “best of breed” product (or ABB in border term) is not necessarily “best fit” in the overall Enterprise Architecture.

Where there are challenges – there are always some solutions…

Obviously, we cannot expect all “bricks” -will really follow the same integration standards – we have to rely on the power of middleware – to connect all loosely coupled “bricks” or ABBs.

… And still, I am still not talking about async or sync – I will come to this…

Let’s go with few examples… I will use an example of Omni-channel Commerce, and some key integrations services around Order Management – primarily around various Sales Order actions like taking an order, tracking status of an order etc.

Figure 2. Omni-channel Commerce

EDA (Event-Driven Architecture) approach

Order Management is not only about “taking an order”. An integral part of the overall Order Fulfilment, is providing “ordered status” to various connected systems – those could trigger some specific action in the connected system or can serve e.g. as an information to the Customer.

Let’s take a look on async EDA PubSub (Publisher-Subscriber)[6] approach…

In this example I will go through the simplest approach when our Backend S/4HANA system is publishing information about Sales Order(s) to the subscribed systems.

Figure 3. Publishing Sales Order Events to the multiple subscribed systems

This example is showing a simple 1:N “broadcasting” pattern (pattern described more in my article)[7]. But even if we would introduce multiple Backend systems (e.g. covering different regions etc.) things would not be much different for N:N pattern.

Figure 4. Multiple systems publishing Sales Order to the multiple subscribed systems

When building integrations with EDA approach, natively by design, it’s decoupled integration

All those Sales Order “statuses” are Events – either new order created, or order has been changed (i.e. quantities, replacing articles, delivery address etc.), or some status of an order has been changed etc.… Using EDA approach to “manage” Events through the ecosystem, either inside the Enterprise, or outside – it’s somehow natural choice.

Target systems or Recipient systems are generally referred as Subscribers – and they are “subscribed” to specific Topic(s).

In the case Event Broker is supporting dynamic Topics, situation may even be more flexible – smart choice of dynamic Topic taxonomy (hierarchical structure, variables etc.) can enable Subscriber to subscribe to only specific subset of Events within one Topic i.e. one Subscriber is interested only for Events when new Sales Order is been created in the specific Sales Organization etc.

Example of the dynamic Topic taxonomy, we can build in SAP AEM (#Advanced Event Mesh), for the Sales Order Events:

/eda/s4/salesorder/v1/{SalesOrganization}/{SalesOrder}/{OrderHeader|OrderItem}/{Create|Change|Delete |…}

In this hierarchy we are defining multiple “levels” within the Topic:

Level_1/Level_2/Level_3/Level_4/Level_5/Level_6/Level_7/Level_8

where:

  • Level 1 and Level 2 describe the domain – in this case our domain is “eda” and source system is “s4”
  • Level 3 is object-type – in this example this is “salesorder”
  • Level 4 is version – in this example this is “v1”
  • Levels 5 and Level 6 are additional dynamic object-types – in this example those are “{SalesOrganization}” are “{SalesOrder}” variables enabling dynamic filtering for Subscriber systems or Subscriber Queues
  • Levels 7 is another dynamic object-type – in the example “{OrderHeader|OrderItem}” is predefined list of values describing type of Event
  • Level 8 is a verb defining the actual action – here those action can be “{Create|Change|Delete |…}”

Beside using variables with dynamic Topics, Subscriber can utilize additional flexibility using “wildcards”, In SAP AEM we have these options:

  • We can subscribe to only Sales Organization starting with “11” using in “11*” in Level 5.
  • Or we can assign for all actions on Order Header using “/>” after Level 7.

The above is only an example – and other rules for building dynamic Topic taxonomy may apply in the different Enterprises.

In general, EDA is more about choreography than orchestration[8][9][10].

Obviously, dynamic Topic taxonomy is a very powerful tool allowing a lot of flexibility for “routing” the traffic from the Publisher(s) to the Subscriber(s)[11]. With smart design of the Topic taxonomy, many things can be done without any further “development” or coding outside of the Event Broker. More info is available in the official SAP AEM page: Get Started with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh[11].

In addition, Recipient systems can choose if they subscribe to the Topic or Queue. My preferred pattern is to use Queue. In this scenario, we will have one or more Queues as actual “Subscribers”, where each Queue will subscribe to one or more Topics (in the case of dynamic Topics, it be also be subsets of the various Topics). Finaly, each Queue is “connected” with the Recipient or Target system.

Various Event Brokers (i.e. SAP Event Mesh, SAP AEM, or non-SAP products as well) may support various ways to communication with Subscribers – it could be AMQP, MQTT, HTTP REST webhook - or even HTTP REST PULL where Target system has to periodically PULL messages form the Queue (this one, I generally do not prefer).

When build smartly, being async with EDA approach (with various SAP or non-SAP products), we can address all Decoupled Integration Architecture requirements:

  • Retry pattern –usually by design;
  • Idempotent –by design;
  • Serialization – depending on partitioning/session configuration, but generally option is available;
  • Guaranteed delivery – depending of the configuration, based on Retry logic and Queue messages, but generally supported;
  • Publish-Subscribe pattern – by design;
  • Async Request-Reply pattern – by design;
  • Queue messages – by design;
  • Distribution pattern – by design;

What does this say to us? EDA approach, by design, brings us scalability and flexibility.

RESTfull API?

But can we follow best practices of decoupling with RESTfull APIs?

Let’s take a look on this Synch Request-Response pattern. Can we use it in Decoupled Integration Architecture? Shell we immediately dismiss it as it is sync?

Wait…

Let’s say, multiple Client systems are invoking sync Sales Order creation in the Backend S/4HANA system …

Figure 5: Multiple Client systems invoking order creation

But we can also have scenarios where middleware is actually routing requests to the different Backend system – e.g. serving different regions or so.

Figure 6: Multiple Client systems invoking order creation, in the multiple Backend systems

In these scenarios, we actually talk about Client system(s) invoking some operation (or command) in the Backend system(s). So, it’s obviously not fully Decoupled, but it does use middleware and can be considered as Loosely-coupled Client-server approach.

We can integrate via API Management Gateway (APIM) and even use façade endpoint – making Client system completely “unaware” of the of the underlaying of Backend system serving RESTfull service Request (and providing the Response).

Furthermore, using APIM Policies we can route traffic to multiple Backend system i.e. serving different regions (or Sales Organization). If needed, we can in between use SAP CPI to adjust payloads etc.

When we talk about Sync Request-Response pattern, where we are with Decoupled Integration Architecture requirements:

  • Retry pattern – no, not by the integration layer – as this is RESTfull API, HTTP(S) Request is invoking sync processing in the Backend system, which is send its HTTP(S) Response. If response is different from http200, sender system can have specific logic for retry mechanism.
  • Idempotent –by design, controlled by the sender;
  • Serialization – by design, controlled by the sender;
  • Guaranteed delivery – no, not by the integration layer – however, sender system will be aware of the failed message based on HTTP(S) Response, and can act on it;
  • Publish-Subscribe pattern – no, as it follows Sync Request-Response pattern;
  • Async Request-Reply pattern – no, as it follows Sync Request-Response pattern;
  • Queue messages – no;
  • Distribution pattern – yes, i.e. beneath API Management Gateway it can route traffic;

Obviously, we can ensure most of the requirements… but not all. No surprise, as we are here talking more about Loosely-coupled Integration Architecture, which is not fully decoupled.

So, is it loosely-coupled or decupled “enough”? I would see “it can be” – but it all depends on the specific use-case… And how we implement it...

The main limitation – no Queue messages capability.

 Sync Request-Response pattern is simply not good in handling “burst” traffic scenario (when we have periodically very high increase in integration message volumes). If we have burst of requests/messages coming to the Backend system, we would have to:

  • Either design overall Backend solution to be able to sustain maximum peak traffic – could be very expensive, as most of the time Backed resources would work with low utilization;
  • Or dynamical scaling of the Backed system(s) to handle peaks – possible with cloud solutions, but also comes with the price;
  • Or Client system(s) will have to accept longer waiting time during the peaks – however, this is not user friendly, and may lead to other technical issues (i.e. sync connection may timeout);

When does Sync Request-Response pattern make sense?

If we run banking transactions – we should normally want to ensure ACID (and keep several operations encapsulated with “one” transaction). Here using Sync Request-Replay pattern is an obvious chose.

But in our examples of the Order Management, some operations may or may not make sense to be run with sync approach.

If we have fairly small Sales Order(s), with 10-20 Products, with no “burst” pattern – submitting it to the Backed system which can perform ATP checks, credit checks and pricing calculation in ”lighting” speed (let’s say S/4HANA) – why not using sync approach?

But what if we have Sales Order(s) with 100, 200 or more Products, with complex Backend pricing and promo calculations? What if we have huge volumes with “burst” pattern? Using async approach for sure looks better option…

It all depends on the specific use-case…

Do we “go” sync or async?

When we talk about Sales Order, as an example, we could have various integration patterns. I am showing few simple examples with some standard APIs and IDOCs – representing both sync and async approach.

Let’s take a look at the “classical” approach…

Figure 7: Splitting Sales Order Taking process for “large” orders

What do we see here:

  • Fully Sync Request-Response pattern using OData API_SALES_ORDER_SRV Sales Order (A2X) for inbound Order Taking requests[11];
  • For “large” orders, async creation of the Sales Order using SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT204 IDOC (previously has been part of the reference integration architecture of SAP Commerce Cloud), without any confirmation weather Backend S/4HANA system has successfully processed the IDOC – this approach can be supplemented with standardSales Order Replication or Standard/custom Sales Order Create Event Notification, allowing Client system to get formal confirmation as well – still, strictly speaking, this is not Async Request-Response pattern, as published Replication or Event Notification is not intended only for the Client system originally creating Sales Order, but to all “interested” Subscribers;

But can we do better with EDA?

Figure 8: Fully (near) real time async approach of Sales Order Taking

Now we have fully Async Request-Response pattern with EDA middleware and few transformations and Value Mappings, of course.

  • Still for smaller message sizes, it will switch to Sync Request-Response pattern toward to Backend S/4HANA system, using OData API_SALES_ORDER_SRV Sales Order (A2X) for inbound Order Taking requests[12] – where Response with Order ID confirmation (or error) is published and respective Subscriber(s) can take it;
  • For “large” orders, fully Async Request-Response pattern using B2B ORDERS and ORDRSP IDOCs – where ORDRSP IDOC with Order ID confirmation is published and respective Subscriber(s) can take it;

What do we achieve with this approach?

First of all, no matter if order is “large” or not, from the perspective of the overall integration process it is always Async Request-Response pattern. We can always set QoS so message is remove from the Topic/Queue only if Sales Order API has been successfully called, or in case of “large” orders IDOC has been successfully created and sent.

In most of the cases, speed would be matching Sync Request-Response pattern, but in all cases Client is not “blocked” until Response has been received.

Event when calling sync Sales Order API, in case of some technical failure, we still have possibility within our EDA middleware to run Retry. This also addresses “burst” pattern.

Now, this is only high-level Solution Concept, not a details design – we may go for other options, and not use B2B IDOCs – but the idea stays the same…

And conclusion is…

Conclusion is straight-forward:

  • We cannot build modern Composable Business (Architecture) without EDA approach, as this is core of the Decoupled Integration Architecture – simple as that!
  • But we should not dismiss RESTfull approach for some use-cases – we just have to make sure we design it in the Loosely-coupled Integration Architecture style.

This article is not evaluating EDA vs. RESTfull – actually it’s about both EDA and RESfull working together. And they can work together…

Still, I would give an “upper edge” to EDA approach because it provides natural way of building scalable and flexible integrations – and this is the “modularity” we must enable (as much as possible) for the Composable Business (Architecture)

Figure 9: Composable Business with “all” enablers

I would not try to put any “golden” rules when to “go” sync and when to go “async” – but I will share few thoughts…

  • When we need to ensure encapsulation of the transactions – go for sync RESTfull API
  • When we have large messages with potentially complex processing in the Recipient system – go for EDA (or async API in general)
  • When we have high volumes and/or “burst” pattern – go for EDA (or async API in general)
  • When we explicitly do not have real time requirements – go for EDA (or async API in general) – even though EDA is quite capably of (near) real time integrations
  • When we are publishing Notification Events, or Data Events, or Decision Events – go for EDA

In all other cases, both RESTfull API and EDA (or async API in general) could do just fine – although we should still evaluate (having the big integration picture in mind) if one or the other approach would fit better on each specific use-case. If we aim for long term scalability and flexibility (and we should) – let’s choose EDA approach.

NOTE: In this article I did not cover streaming and ETL integration styles (i.e. for Big Data). But we will see in the forthcoming articles, that streaming and ETL integration style can be very much supported by EDA tools and approach, in general.

Acknowledgment

*) Intro photo Joshua Sortino on Unsplash

**) This article uses SAP Business Technology Platform Solution Diagrams & Icons as per SAP Terms of Use governing the use of these SAP Materials (please note, newer version of the Solution Diagrams & Icons, as well as Terms of Use, might be in place after the publication of this article).

More guidelines on Solution Diagrams & Icons can be found in this article by Bertram Ganz.

References

[1] SAP Blog: What is Composable Architecture? - SAP Community

[2] Gartner: The Future of Business Is Composable - Gartner Keynote

[3] SAP Blog from @mariafay: What is a composable enterprise? And who is the co... - SAP Community

[4] Solace: Why it's Time to Event Enable your Integration | Solace

[5] SAP Blog from @fjaviergar07: Composable Architecture: A Practical Example - SAP Community

[6] Wikipedia: Publish–subscribe pattern

[7] SAP Blog: How to build an Integration Architecture for the I... - SAP Community

[8] Solace: Microservices Choreography vs Orchestration Overview

[9] IBM: Event-driven architectures with Kafka and Kafka Streams - IBM Developer

[10] Choreography and orchestration | Serverless Land

[11] SAP AEM: Understanding Topics

[12] SAP Blog on Omni-channel: Building Integration Architecture for the Omni-cha... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Success Factors - Employee Central - Foundation Objects and Tables Explained
entry.content: Awesome!

entry.title: Update a Key figure for End date of order by subtracting the value of GR processing time
entry.content:
I need to get the Ends dates of Confirmed Receipts updated, by subtracting the GR processing time with reference to the Product Master, for the following two objects in Planning Book of SAP APO SNP:

1. Process Order

2. Purchase Requisition

entry.title: Story Calculations in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

Based on user given input in the variable as date, i have display 15year sales data as below

Could someone please give an idea on how can i achieve this?

entry.title: SAP Loves ABAP (Again) — Insights from Constellation Research
entry.content:
At SAP TechEd 2024, SAP made a noteworthy announcement that has reignited interest in ABAP: support for ABAP has now been added to SAP Build. This addition brings together ABAP, pro-code (like Java), and low-code/no-code tools in one cohesive platform, empowering developers to modernize and build business applications with unparalleled flexibility.

Constellation Research recently published a research report, SAP Loves ABAP (Again), which dives into the impact of this development and highlights the ongoing significance of ABAP for SAP customers. The report traces ABAP's evolution from its roots in the mainframe days to the versatile, business-focused programming language it is today.

Constellation’s report explores how ABAP’s integration with SAP Build strengthens SAP’s tech stack by merging different coding paradigms into a single platform, ultimately allowing SAP customers to keep pace with changing business demands.

Check out Constellation Research's full report to understand how SAP is reaffirming its commitment to ABAP and what this means for developers, particularly in the SAP Build community!

entry.title: Re: Usage of Function module /SPE/DELV_RESET_DIST_STATUS to change the delivery distribution status
entry.content:
Hi damaverick,

This process is primarily needed when users accidentally delete the EWM delivery, as no changes can be made to the LE delivery until the distribution status is adjusted. It's useful for handling one-off cases and supports thorough integration testing in lower environments.

Thanks,
Prasanna

entry.title: Re: I have a issues reagrding the VAS KIT to stock in EWM- ERROR WHILE GENERATING OR UPDATING DELIVE
entry.content: You need to implement SAP note 3443303

entry.title: Re: I have a issues reagrding the VAS KIT to stock in EWM...
entry.content: Implement SAP Note 3443303

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Public without tax engine
entry.content:
Regarding the implementation of SAP S4HANA Cloud Public edition, it is known that for Brazilian localization purposes customers must rely on external tax engines for proper taxation, based on each business cenário.  However, more than once the question about the necessity to hire this additional services - tax engine SaaS - came into the conversation table.

In one every specific scenario, a customer with low transaction volume (with high service invoice values), invoicing directly (and manually) in the Sao Paulo's cityhall website, asked if it would be possible to have S4HC Public WITHOUT a tax engine. Their idea is to post manually in FI the receivables after invoicing manually and, from the inbound perspective, to post also manually all the values when processing MIGO_MIRO. In this way, determinations wouldn't be required and, so, the tax engine.

This is a new scenario for me and I would like to receive guidance from the experts. Does anybody have implemented (or at least an opinion about) the possibility of avoiding a Tax Engine contract to operate S4HC Public?

Thanks a lot 

😉

André Mascaro

entry.title: Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE...
entry.content:
I SAID TEST IT USING ODBC 19 to SEE if it works.

If it works ask Microsoft to fix their OLE DB 19 client to generate the same SQL as ODBC 18.

In CR 9 and since R&D stopped building in "hacks" to "fix" third party Client issues, if there is a problem with MS's OLE DB 19 client MS has to fix it.

And Yes if you test updating one report and redirecting CR to use the new ODBC 19 Client it's the ONLY way to fix all of your reports.

entry.title: Re: CR2020 viewer print output is incorrect when PrintMod...
entry.content: Look at my test app, it's all there and use POC in your code to select the printer.

entry.title: Intercompany credit memo
entry.content:
Hi, what would be the copy control VTFA settings for intercompany credit memo (CR to IG). 

Trying to create the intercompany billing doc with reference to credit memo request.

Is there any standard formula routine in copy control for this process?

entry.title: Re: Parallel processing made easy
entry.content: @matt , I have a BAPI that I need to execute for multiple records and it only updates one record at a time.  If I were to use parallel processing and any of those parallel BAPI calls happened to fail (aka return error messages), would I still be able to commit (via BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT) the records where the BAPI call was successful without committing the ones that failed?  Or would I be stuck rolling everything back?

entry.title: Re: CDS view with Change Data Capture(CDC) for Replication Flow – Part 1
entry.content:
Hi @Cocquerel , 

@albertosimeoni has already addressed the first part of your question.

The 2 different CDC annotation A and B in the blog is dependent on the complexity of CDS view. If it's a simple projection view you use the automatic annotation (A), if it's a complex CDS view it's the mapping annotation(B). Both the annotations use the same framework(CDC). 

Best Regards,

Sayani

entry.title: Re: In Dispute management - Case type F_DM is missing
entry.content: following

entry.title: Re: [Error] BDC clearing partial vendor field not input
entry.content: Can you recommend resolution to fix it? I need posting FI Clearing partial payment

entry.title: Re: GRC Tuesdays: Hidden Gems – SAP Watch List Screening for Simplified Due Diligence and Compliance
entry.content:
Thanks for highlighting the list of supported documents.  It seems return sales documents and return deliveries are not supported.  Eg, CBAR and LR2 documents are not showing up in the list of doc types that could be activated.  What's the rationale for excluding these?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE...
entry.content: As I already said: Tests with Microsoft ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server were successful (and that report did not need any modification). It creates a command like this: "DBNAME"."dbo"."STORED_PROCEDURE_NAME";1 '02-14-2023'

entry.title: Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE...
entry.content: However, as an outsider, I don't understand why Microsoft and not SAP CR should fix anything. According to SQL Profiler, the same metadata queries are first executed by CR runtime with both OLE DB drivers (i.e. version 18 and 19), with the same results. But CR runtime then generates an invalid SQL statement to query the data only with version 19. Does CR runtime or the OLE DB driver generate this invalid query? I would guess the CR runtime. As you said, the CR runtime generates the generic SQL syntax, which is not valid for SQL Server.

entry.title: Re: steps to configure p.o for outbound IDOC
entry.content: Nice, congratulations!

entry.title: Blocked Transactions SAP ME / HANA
entry.content:
Hi 

We have some problems with SAP ME and SAP MII, for a few moments, the system becomes slow

We review in the SAP Hana Cockpit tool, and we can see some Blocked Transactions, the main blockeds are following: 

We can see this top 3 queries in the tool

1.- UPDATE SFC_ROUTER SET QTY=?
WHERE HANDLE=? 

2.-SELECT MODIFIED_DATE_TIME,
START_DATE_TIME,
STRT,
HANDLE
FROM RESOURCE_TIME_LOG
WHERE RESOURCE_BO=?
AND ACTIVE=? forUPDATE 

3.- SELECT MODIFIED_DATE_TIME,
START_DATE_TIME,
HANDLE,
RESOURCE_STATE,
STRT
FROM RESOURCE_TIME_LOG
WHERE RESOURCE_BO=?
AND ACTIVE=? forUPDATE 

One question, This queries are standard?

Any idea to identify where it is executed from? 

Regards

entry.title: Re: batch determination based on WM storage type stock po...
entry.content: Hello, I'm experiencing the same issue: the standard lot determination in the delivery doesn't account for the EWM storage type. Any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE DB Driver 19 for SQL Server" (MS
entry.content: Test with ODBC 19, we know 18 works, to see if it generates the correct SQL. CR dynamically generates the Syntax based on the Client DLL used, which is why you can see differences between OLE DB and ODBC

entry.title: Unable to find the table information
entry.content:
Dear team,

Client is using S4CRM, they need a custom report which have lead id with status , opportunity id with status, once opportunity converted to sales quotation, then quotation id, sales quotation converted to sales order then sales order id should be in the report.

When I found table in S4CRM that is CRMS4D_OPPT_H which save the lead and opportunity information but not the sales questions and sales orders, sales quotations and sales orders will be stored in VBAK standard  table.

How can I combine these tables and get the complete information about the transactional data, could you please help on the same 

 

Regards

Anwar

entry.title: Re: FREE TRIAL
entry.content: Did you solve the problem? I am able to activate 1909 with GCP

entry.title: Issue with Bulk Requisition Creation and Excel Copy-Paste for Stock Transfer
entry.content:
we need to create manual reservations through requisitions, but we are encountering the following challenges. 

  • Bulk Requisition Creation: We are unable to create requisitions in bulk for the 650 items that need to be transferred. The system does not seem to support this functionality for our use case.
  • Excel Copy-Paste Issue: Even though we have the data in the correct format in Excel, the system is not allowing us to copy and paste the information into the requisition fields.

entry.title: Re: Hello Sydney
entry.content:
I began SAP life by taking over support for Australia’s first live R/3 site on 2.1E at the ripe old age of 17.  That qualifies me as a dinosaur.

Stegosaurus was already my favourite dinosaur before Gary Larson gave the official name to its tail - the Thagomizer. Defo sealed the deal.

entry.title: Re: Where is the log file of Add-On?
entry.content: https://help.sap.com/doc/011000358700000883202009e/8.82/en-US/HowTo_Logging_Tracing_88.pdf

entry.title: Re: Unable to insert data getting ENTITY_ALREADY_EXISTS
entry.content:
If you are trying to do some validations using srv.before event and creating records/data using the same event, then you would be facing this issue.

Please make sure if there are any validations before record insertion, use srv.before and for Insert query use srv.on event, that should solve your problem.

 

Shiromani Soni

entry.title: Re: AMDP error -SQLSCRIPT: sql syntax error: incorrect syntax near "/"
entry.content: Make sure your using only UPPER CASE characters. Using lower case characters with "(Double Quotes) will show as error only.

entry.title: Will this work in case of restoration 10 days old backup of consolidated database
entry.content:
Hello experts,

My question is similar to the previously asked questions. But just to get clear idea, I am writing again. Below is the case.

Case - I want to restore the 10 days old backup of the consolidated database and want to repull the journal entries from all the branch databases. Will it work ? What steps I had to follow if anything are there.

entry.title: Username and Password on SAP Netweaver
entry.content: I was able to connect 1909 to GCP. Application SAP NetWeaver Administration connected successfully to SAP S4/HANA 1909 FAA client 100. What username and password shall I use and where do I extract that information from? Your help is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Using RAP Generator, Put Operation fails
entry.content: The issue has been resolved for us after renaming one of the column field in the table

entry.title: Multiple table entries Creation in single object page Without Navigation to another page
entry.content:
Hi Community ,

We got a requirement in RAP(ABAP Environment ) which contains header data, multiple purchase order creation table and attachment table.

In Purchase order table , we need to create multiple PO in same screen itself without navigating to next screen/page (screenshots are added ) .We have trying to developing this screen using Managed Scenario. Pls guide us . 

 

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model   SAP S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition 

@peterpersiel

entry.title: Error Importing content in SAP Build Work Zone - SAP Mobile Start with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I’m setting up SAP Mobile Start with SAP S/4HANA Cloud in SAP Build Work Zone.
Tutorial: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/Setting-Up-SAP-Build-Work-Zone-standard-edition-and-SAP-Mobile-Start-with-SAP-S-4HANA

The problem is that when I import content into SAP Build Work Zone, it doesn’t bring any components.

When I check in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using the “Display Launchpad Content Exposure Logs” app, I see the scenario is called correctly.

Here is how I have the content provider configured:

This is how I have the destinations set up in BTP:
Design-time destination

Runtime tiles

Runtime apps

This is the SAP S/4HANA Cloud role exposed in BTP.

Does anyone have an idea where the error might be or what configuration point I should check?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Need inputs on how to change Field names while using...
entry.content:
Ryan-Crosby has the right Answer, but as this is a quite basic question I was wondering if you have checked the demo programs? They are quite useful 

Search for programs like : SALV_DEMO*

entry.title: Re: New account group defined not display in FS00
entry.content: The account group does not appear in the existing list on the left side of the screen. However, when creating a new G/L account, the account group can be selected from the "Type/Description" tab, as it is available in the dropdown list for selection.
